title: June 26, 2026 public safety-source status
summary: Most user-visible safety sources remained available on June 26, 2026. The main public-source changes were an FDA notice that MAUDE data is planned to move into a newer adverse-event platform later in 2026, and AEMPS notices about maintenance that may affect website freshness and a scheduled application outage in July. Current public access for those sources remained available during this check. [FDA MDR Data Files](https://www.fda.gov/medical-devices/medical-device-reporting-mdr-how-report-medical-device-problems/mdr-data-files) [AEMPS](https://www.aemps.gob.es/) [AEMPS Sede](https://sede.aemps.gob.es/)
updates: FDA said on its MDR Data Files page that MAUDE data will move into its Adverse Event Monitoring System sometime in 2026; public MAUDE access was still available on June 26, 2026. [FDA MDR Data Files](https://www.fda.gov/medical-devices/medical-device-reporting-mdr-how-report-medical-device-problems/mdr-data-files)

AEMPS said its corporate website stopped receiving new content updates starting June 9, 2026 for maintenance, and its electronic services posted a planned outage for July 18, 2026 from 14:00 to 23:00. [AEMPS](https://www.aemps.gob.es/) [AEMPS Sede](https://sede.aemps.gob.es/)
no_major_changes: No significant public-source access or availability changes were found for FDA Recall, FDA CDRH Recalls, FDA Safety Communications, BfArM, Swissmedic, MHRA, TGA DAEN, TGA DRAC, Health Canada DHPR, Canada Recall, ANVISA, PMDA, ANSM, or the English NMPA pages.
